claim.hypotheses.explanation: Extracted lists main-effect hypotheses (efficient governments, tight cultures -> better outcomes), but the reference hypothesis is specifically about the negative interaction between tightness and efficiency on infection rate.

claim.hypotheses_location.explanation: Extracted places the hypothesis in the Results (p. 10), while the reference locates it on p. 3, second paragraph.

claim.statement.explanation: Extracted states that efficient governments and tight cultures have better COVID-19 outcomes, which captures the core meaning, but omits the emphasis on the significant interaction reported in the reference.

claim.statement_location.explanation: Extracted cites the Introduction (p. 1), whereas the reference specifies p. 7, first paragraph.

claim.study_type.explanation: Both identify the study as Observational.

data.source.explanation: Extracted vaguely notes COVID-19 statistics from 141 nations; the reference specifies detailed sources (ECDC, World Bank GEI, Gelfand tightness, IMF GDP, World Bank Gini, CIA median age).

data.wave_or_subset.explanation: Extracted gives a March 21–30, 2020 window not reflected in the reference, which lists multiple years by source and methodological filtering (post 1 case/million) rather than that specific window.

data.sample_size.explanation: Extracted reports counts of cases/deaths; the reference states the sample size as 141 nations.

data.unit_of_analysis.explanation: Both indicate the unit of analysis is the nation.

data.access_details.explanation: Extracted says data and code are online; the reference specifies OSF (https://osf.io/pc4ef/). Core meaning (publicly available) is preserved but lacks the exact link.

data.notes.explanation: Extracted mentions using World Bank GEI and Gelfand tightness index, aligning with reference, but omits other measurement details (e.g., mortality calculation, GDP/Gini indexing, efficiency dimensions).

method.description.explanation: Extracted summarizes regression analyses relating tightness and government efficiency to infection and mortality with controls, consistent with the reference’s broad description, but omits specifics like growth-rate modeling.

method.steps.explanation: Extracted lists general steps (collect data, compute outcomes, regress with controls) but misses key procedures in the reference (post-threshold filtering, log transforms, weighting by days, distribution checks).

method.models.explanation: Extracted mentions robust and logistic regression; the reference specifies weighted ordinary least squares regression.

method.outcome_variable.explanation: Extracted includes infection rate and mortality likelihood; the reference highlights infection rate as the modeled outcome in the main specification.

method.independent_variables.explanation: Both list government efficiency and cultural tightness.

method.control_variables.explanation: Both include economic development (GDP per capita), inequality (Gini), and median age.

results.summary.explanation: Extracted emphasizes main effects of tightness and efficiency lowering infection and mortality; the reference’s main result is the significant negative interaction between tightness and efficiency on infection rate.

results.numerical_results[0].outcome_nam.explanation: Extracted labels the outcome as 'COVID-19 infection rate' whereas the reference entry’s label is not specified (NA).

results.numerical_results[0].value.explanation: Extracted value is -0.15 vs. the reference -0.17; close but not matching.

results.numerical_results[0].statistical.explanation: Both indicate the result is statistically significant/true.

results.numerical_results[1].outcome_nam.explanation: Extracted: 'Government efficiency on infection rate' vs. reference: 't-statistic'.

results.numerical_results[1].value.explanation: Extracted coefficient (-0.19) does not match the reference t-statistic (-2.23).

results.numerical_results[1].p_value.explanation: Extracted provides p = 0.006, whereas the reference lists NA.

results.numerical_results[1].statistical.explanation: Extracted marks as true; the reference lists NA.

results.numerical_results[1].direction.explanation: Extracted indicates negative; the reference lists NA.

metadata.original_paper_title.explanation: Extracted title differs from the reference title.

metadata.original_paper_code.explanation: Extracted is vague; the reference provides the exact OSF link (https://osf.io/pc4ef/).

metadata.original_paper_data.explanation: Extracted is vague; the reference provides the exact OSF link (https://osf.io/pc4ef/).
